accesskeys.why: Duplicate accesskey values make keyboard shortcuts ambiguous, so users trigger the wrong control.
accesskeys.fix: Give every accesskey attribute on the page a unique value, or remove redundant accesskey attributes.

aria-allowed-attr.why: ARIA attributes not allowed on an element's role are ignored or misread by screen readers, hiding intended state.
aria-allowed-attr.fix: Remove or replace ARIA attributes that don't match the element's role (e.g. drop aria-checked from role="button"; use aria-pressed instead).

aria-allowed-role.why: An ARIA role that isn't valid for the element it's on is ignored or misannounced by assistive tech.
aria-allowed-role.fix: Use a role permitted for that element, or switch to the native element that implies the role.

aria-command-name.why: Buttons, links, and menuitems with no accessible name are announced only as their role, leaving screen reader users guessing.
aria-command-name.fix: Add inner text, aria-label, or aria-labelledby to every element with role="button", "link", or "menuitem".

aria-conditional-attr.why: ARIA attributes used outside the conditions their role allows convey wrong or conflicting state to assistive tech.
aria-conditional-attr.fix: Only use conditional ARIA attributes as the role spec allows (e.g. don't set aria-checked on a native <input type="checkbox">; let the checked state speak).

aria-deprecated-role.why: Deprecated ARIA roles may be ignored by assistive technologies, so the element's purpose is lost.
aria-deprecated-role.fix: Replace deprecated roles (e.g. role="directory") with a current supported role or native HTML element.

aria-dialog-name.why: A dialog without an accessible name gives screen reader users no context about what just opened.
aria-dialog-name.fix: Add aria-label or aria-labelledby (pointing at the dialog's heading) to elements with role="dialog" or role="alertdialog".

aria-hidden-body.why: aria-hidden="true" on <body> hides the entire page from assistive technology.

aria-hidden-focus.why: Focusable elements inside aria-hidden containers can receive keyboard focus while being invisible to screen readers.
aria-hidden-focus.fix: Add tabindex="-1" or disabled to focusable descendants of aria-hidden="true" containers, or remove aria-hidden.

aria-input-field-name.why: ARIA input fields (combobox, listbox, searchbox, etc.) without a name are announced without any purpose.
aria-input-field-name.fix: Add aria-label or aria-labelledby to every element with an ARIA input role like combobox, listbox, or searchbox.

aria-meter-name.why: A meter with no accessible name reads as an unlabeled value, meaningless to screen reader users.
aria-meter-name.fix: Add aria-label or aria-labelledby to elements with role="meter" describing what the value measures.

aria-progressbar-name.why: An unnamed progressbar announces a percentage with no indication of what is progressing.
aria-progressbar-name.fix: Add aria-label or aria-labelledby to elements with role="progressbar".

aria-prohibited-attr.why: ARIA attributes prohibited on an element's role (like aria-label on generic text) are ignored, so the intended name never reaches users.
aria-prohibited-attr.fix: Remove prohibited attributes or add an appropriate role (e.g. don't put aria-label on a plain <span>/<div> without a role).

aria-required-attr.why: Roles missing their required ARIA attributes leave assistive tech without essential state like checked or value.
aria-required-attr.fix: Add the role's required attributes (e.g. aria-checked for role="checkbox", aria-valuenow for role="slider").

aria-required-children.why: Composite roles without their required child roles break the structure screen readers use to navigate (e.g. a list with no items).
aria-required-children.fix: Ensure roles like list, tablist, or menu directly contain their required children (listitem, tab, menuitem).

aria-required-parent.why: Child roles outside their required parent (e.g. a listitem with no list) are orphaned and misannounced.
aria-required-parent.fix: Nest roles like listitem, tab, or treeitem inside their required parent role (list, tablist, tree).

aria-roles.why: Invalid or misspelled role values are ignored, so the element falls back to no semantics at all.
aria-roles.fix: Use only valid ARIA role names in role attributes (check spelling against the WAI-ARIA spec, e.g. role="button" not role="buttton").

aria-text.why: role="text" on elements containing focusable descendants hides those controls' semantics from screen readers.
aria-text.fix: Remove role="text" from elements that contain focusable or interactive children, or move the controls outside.

aria-toggle-field-name.why: Unnamed toggle fields (checkboxes, switches, radios) announce a state with no clue what is being toggled.
aria-toggle-field-name.fix: Add aria-label or aria-labelledby to elements with role="checkbox", "switch", "radio", or "menuitemcheckbox".

aria-tooltip-name.why: A tooltip with no accessible name provides no information, defeating its purpose for assistive tech users.
aria-tooltip-name.fix: Give elements with role="tooltip" inner text or an aria-label/aria-labelledby.

aria-treeitem-name.why: Unnamed tree items make tree navigation meaningless for screen reader users.
aria-treeitem-name.fix: Add inner text, aria-label, or aria-labelledby to every element with role="treeitem".

aria-valid-attr.why: Misspelled or nonexistent ARIA attributes are ignored entirely, so the intended accessibility info never applies.
aria-valid-attr.fix: Correct ARIA attribute names to valid ones (e.g. aria-labelledby, not aria-labeledby).

aria-valid-attr-value.why: ARIA attributes with invalid values (e.g. aria-labelledby pointing at a missing ID) silently fail, breaking the intended semantics.
aria-valid-attr-value.fix: Fix ARIA attribute values: reference existing element IDs and use only allowed tokens (e.g. aria-expanded="true|false").

autocomplete-valid.why: Invalid autocomplete values prevent browsers and assistive tech from autofilling fields users struggle to type.
autocomplete-valid.fix: Use valid autocomplete tokens from the HTML spec (e.g. autocomplete="email", "given-name", "postal-code").

bf-cache.why: Pages blocked from the back/forward cache reload from scratch on back navigation instead of restoring instantly.
bf-cache.fix: Remove unload event handlers, avoid Cache-Control: no-store on the document, and close open connections (WebSocket/IndexedDB) in pagehide.

bootup-time.why: Heavy JS execution on load blocks the main thread and delays interactivity.
bootup-time.fix: Reduce JS shipped (see unused-javascript), avoid large synchronous work in module top-level scope, defer non-critical hydration.

button-name.why: A button with no accessible name is announced just as 'button', leaving screen reader users unable to know its action.
button-name.fix: Add visible text inside the <button>, or aria-label/aria-labelledby (and title/alt for icon-only buttons).

bypass.why: Without a way to skip repeated navigation, keyboard users must tab through every header link on each page.
bypass.fix: Add a skip link as the first focusable element (e.g. Skip to content) or proper landmarks/headings.

cache-insight.why: Static assets with short or missing cache lifetimes force repeat visitors to re-download unchanged files.
cache-insight.fix: Serve static assets with Cache-Control: max-age=31536000, immutable and use hashed filenames so updates bust the cache.

canonical.why: Missing/incorrect canonical tags can cause duplicate-content SEO issues.
canonical.fix: Add a self-referencing <link rel="canonical"> per page pointing to its own preferred URL.

charset.why: A missing/late charset declaration can cause the browser to re-parse the document or misrender text.
charset.fix: Add <meta charset="utf-8"> as the first element inside <head>, before any other content.

clickjacking-mitigation.why: Without frame-ancestors or X-Frame-Options, attackers can embed the page in an iframe and hijack clicks.
clickjacking-mitigation.fix: Send Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'none' (or X-Frame-Options: DENY) unless framing is required.

cls-culprits-insight.why: This pinpoints the specific elements and causes (unsized media, injected content, web fonts) behind your layout shifts.
cls-culprits-insight.fix: Fix each named culprit: add dimensions to unsized media, reserve space for injected content, and preload fonts with size-adjusted fallbacks.

color-contrast.why: Low-contrast text is unreadable for many users.
color-contrast.fix: Ensure text/background contrast ratio ≥ 4.5:1 (3:1 for large text); check with the Lighthouse-flagged elements directly.

crawlable-anchors.why: Links without a valid href (e.g. href="#" or javascript:) can't be followed by crawlers, hiding linked pages from search engines.
crawlable-anchors.fix: Give every <a> a crawlable href pointing to a real URL; use <button> instead of anchors for JavaScript-only actions.

csp-xss.why: Missing/weak Content-Security-Policy leaves the site open to XSS.
csp-xss.fix: Add a CSP header restricting script-src to 'self' plus explicit hosts/nonces; avoid 'unsafe-inline' where possible.

cumulative-layout-shift.why: Unexpected layout shifts move content under the user's finger, causing misclicks and a janky feel.
cumulative-layout-shift.fix: Set explicit width/height on images/videos/embeds, reserve space for ads and dynamic content, and use font-display: optional or size-adjusted font fallbacks.

definition-list.why: A malformed <dl> (containing non dt/dd children) breaks the term-definition pairing screen readers announce.
definition-list.fix: Ensure <dl> contains only properly ordered <dt>/<dd> pairs (optionally wrapped in <div>), with no other direct children.

deprecations.why: Deprecated APIs will eventually be removed from browsers, so pages relying on them risk breaking without warning.
deprecations.fix: Check the DevTools Issues panel for each deprecation warning and migrate to the recommended replacement API.

dlitem.why: <dt>/<dd> elements outside a <dl> lose their term-definition semantics for assistive tech.
dlitem.fix: Wrap every <dt> and <dd> in a parent <dl> element.

doctype.why: A missing or malformed doctype triggers quirks mode, causing inconsistent layout and rendering across browsers.
doctype.fix: Add <!DOCTYPE html> as the very first line of the document, before the <html> tag.

document-latency-insight.why: Redirects, a slow server response, or an uncompressed HTML document delay every other resource on the page.
document-latency-insight.fix: Eliminate redirects to the main document, get server response under 600 ms (caching/CDN), and enable gzip or brotli compression on the HTML.

document-title.why: A missing or generic <title> hurts SEO and browser tab identification.
document-title.fix: Give every page a unique, descriptive <title>.

dom-size.why: Very large DOM trees slow style/layout recalculation and increase memory.
dom-size.fix: Virtualize long lists, avoid deeply nested wrapper divs, paginate instead of rendering everything at once.

dom-size-insight.why: A large DOM increases memory, slows style/layout calculations, and makes every interaction more expensive.
dom-size-insight.fix: Reduce total nodes (aim under ~1,500), flatten deep nesting, and virtualize long lists instead of rendering them fully.

duplicate-id-aria.why: Duplicate IDs referenced by ARIA attributes make aria-labelledby/aria-describedby resolve to the wrong element.
duplicate-id-aria.fix: Make every ID referenced by ARIA attributes (aria-labelledby, aria-controls, etc.) unique on the page.

duplicated-javascript-insight.why: The same modules bundled into multiple chunks ship redundant bytes that slow download and parse.
duplicated-javascript-insight.fix: Deduplicate dependencies (npm dedupe) and configure your bundler's splitChunks/manualChunks to share common modules across bundles.

efficient-animated-content.why: Large animated GIFs are far heavier than video for the same content.
efficient-animated-content.fix: Replace GIFs with <video autoplay loop muted playsinline> using MP4/WebM.

empty-heading.why: Empty headings confuse screen reader navigation — users jump to a heading and hear nothing.
empty-heading.fix: Add text to the heading or remove the empty <h1>-<h6> element entirely.

errors-in-console.why: Console errors indicate unresolved problems like failed requests or broken JavaScript that can degrade the user experience.
errors-in-console.fix: Open DevTools, reproduce each logged error, and fix its root cause (broken resources, uncaught exceptions, blocked requests).

first-contentful-paint.why: A slow FCP means users stare at a blank screen with no sign the page is loading.
first-contentful-paint.fix: Eliminate render-blocking CSS/JS, inline critical CSS, cut server response time (TTFB), and use font-display: swap so text paints immediately.

font-display.why: Fonts without font-display can cause invisible text while loading (FOIT).
font-display.fix: Add font-display: swap (or optional) in @font-face, and consider self-hosting + preloading critical fonts.

font-display-insight.why: Fonts without font-display keep text invisible while they load, delaying FCP and LCP.
font-display-insight.fix: Add font-display: swap (or optional) to every @font-face, and preload critical font files.

forced-reflow-insight.why: JS that reads layout properties after writing styles forces synchronous reflows that block the main thread.
forced-reflow-insight.fix: Batch DOM reads before writes, avoid querying offsetHeight/getBoundingClientRect inside loops, and use requestAnimationFrame to schedule style changes.

form-field-multiple-labels.why: Multiple labels on one form field cause screen readers to announce inconsistent or unexpected names.
form-field-multiple-labels.fix: Use exactly one <label for> per field; merge extra text into that label or use aria-describedby for hints.

frame-title.why: Frames without titles force screen reader users to enter them blindly to learn their content.
frame-title.fix: Add a descriptive title attribute to every <iframe> and <frame> (e.g. title="Payment form").

geolocation-on-start.why: Requesting geolocation on page load without context confuses users and leads most to deny the permission.
geolocation-on-start.fix: Only call navigator.geolocation after a user gesture (e.g. clicking a 'Use my location' button) that explains why it's needed.

has-hsts.why: Without HSTS, first requests can go over HTTP, leaving users open to protocol-downgrade attacks.
has-hsts.fix: Send Strict-Transport-Security with a long max-age (e.g. max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains) on HTTPS responses.

heading-order.why: Skipped heading levels (h2 to h4) break the outline screen reader users navigate by.
heading-order.fix: Use heading levels in sequential order (h1 → h2 → h3) without skipping; style with CSS, not heading level.

hreflang.why: Incorrect hreflang confuses search engines about localized variants.
hreflang.fix: Ensure hreflang tags are valid ISO language/region codes and reciprocal across variants.

html-has-lang.why: Without a lang attribute, screen readers guess the language and may mispronounce the whole page.
html-has-lang.fix: Add a lang attribute to the root element, e.g. <html lang="en">.

html-lang-valid.why: An invalid lang value makes screen readers fall back to a default voice, mispronouncing content.
html-lang-valid.fix: Set <html lang> to a valid BCP 47 code (e.g. lang="en" or lang="en-US", not lang="english").

html-xml-lang-mismatch.why: When lang and xml:lang disagree, assistive technologies may pick the wrong language for pronunciation.
html-xml-lang-mismatch.fix: Make the lang and xml:lang attributes on <html> specify the same base language.

http-status-code.why: Pages returning 4xx/5xx status codes may be dropped from search indexes since crawlers treat them as unavailable.
http-status-code.fix: Fix the server or routing so the page returns a 2xx status code (or a proper 301 redirect to its canonical URL).

identical-links-same-purpose.why: Links with identical text pointing to different URLs are indistinguishable to screen reader users.
identical-links-same-purpose.fix: Give links with different destinations distinct accessible names (text, aria-label, or visually hidden text).

image-alt.why: Screen readers can't describe images without alt text.
image-alt.fix: Add descriptive alt on all content ; use alt="" for purely decorative images.

image-aspect-ratio.why: Rendering images at an aspect ratio different from their natural one makes them appear stretched or squashed.
image-aspect-ratio.fix: Set width/height (or CSS aspect-ratio / object-fit) so each image's displayed dimensions match its natural aspect ratio.

image-delivery-insight.why: Oversized, poorly compressed, or legacy-format images waste bandwidth and delay LCP.
image-delivery-insight.fix: Serve images in AVIF/WebP, resize to actual display dimensions with srcset, raise compression, and lazy-load offscreen images.

image-redundant-alt.why: Alt text repeating adjacent text makes screen readers announce the same content twice.
image-redundant-alt.fix: Use alt="" for decorative/redundant images, or make the alt text add information the surrounding text lacks.

image-size-responsive.why: Images served below their displayed size appear blurry, especially on high-DPI screens.
image-size-responsive.fix: Serve images at least as large as their rendered size times the device pixel ratio, using srcset/sizes for responsive variants.

inp-breakdown-insight.why: This breaks the slowest interaction into input delay, processing, and presentation so you can see what to fix.
inp-breakdown-insight.fix: Cut the longest phase: reduce input delay by breaking up long tasks, shorten processing in event handlers, and shrink presentation delay by reducing DOM/rendering work.

input-button-name.why: An input button without discernible text gives screen reader users no idea what it does.
input-button-name.fix: Add a value attribute to <input type="button">/<input type="submit">, or an aria-label.

input-image-alt.why: Image buttons without alt text are announced as 'unlabeled button', hiding their action.
input-image-alt.fix: Add a descriptive alt attribute to every <input type="image"> (e.g. alt="Search").

inspector-issues.why: Issues logged to the DevTools Issues panel (mixed content, CORS failures, cookie problems) signal real breakage or security risks.
inspector-issues.fix: Open the DevTools Issues panel and resolve each reported issue, such as upgrading mixed-content URLs to HTTPS and fixing CORS headers.

interaction-to-next-paint.why: A high INP means taps and clicks feel laggy because the next frame after interaction is delayed.
interaction-to-next-paint.fix: Break up long tasks, debounce heavy input handlers, yield to the main thread (scheduler.yield/setTimeout), and reduce DOM size and rendering work per interaction.

interactive.why: A late Time to Interactive means the page looks ready but ignores or lags on user input.
interactive.fix: Reduce JavaScript payload via code-splitting and tree-shaking, defer third-party scripts, and minimize main-thread work during load.

is-crawlable.why: A noindex directive or blocking robots rule prevents search engines from indexing the page, so it can't appear in results.
is-crawlable.fix: Remove noindex/none from the robots meta tag and X-Robots-Tag header (and unblock the URL in robots.txt) for pages that should rank.

is-on-https.why: Non-HTTPS pages fail modern security and browser feature requirements.
is-on-https.fix: Serve all resources over HTTPS; most hosts (Vercel/Netlify/Cloudflare) do this by default — check for mixed-content http:// asset URLs.

label.why: Unlabeled form fields are announced without purpose, making forms unusable for screen reader users.
label.fix: Associate each input with a <label for="id"> element, or add aria-label/aria-labelledby.

label-content-name-mismatch.why: Voice-control users say the visible label; if the accessible name differs, the command fails.
label-content-name-mismatch.fix: Make the accessible name (aria-label/aria-labelledby) start with the element's visible text.

landmark-one-main.why: Screen reader users rely on a <main> landmark to skip straight to page content.
landmark-one-main.fix: Wrap the primary content in a single <main> element (not inside <header>/<footer>/<nav>).

largest-contentful-paint.why: A slow LCP means the main content (hero image/heading) takes too long to appear, so the page feels slow.
largest-contentful-paint.fix: Preload the hero image (fetchpriority="high"), cut server response time, remove render-blocking CSS/JS, and serve the LCP image compressed in AVIF/WebP from a CDN.

largest-contentful-paint-element.why: Identifies which element is your LCP — usually a hero image or heading.
largest-contentful-paint-element.fix: Preload the LCP image (<link rel=preload as=image>), avoid client-side rendering it, ensure it's not behind a lazy-load.

layout-shift-elements.why: Elements that shift after paint hurt CLS and user experience.
layout-shift-elements.fix: Set explicit width/height (or aspect-ratio) on images/embeds/ads, reserve space for web fonts with font-display + fallback metrics, avoid inserting content above existing content.

layout-shifts.why: The largest layout shifts identify exactly which elements jumped and disrupted the user.
layout-shifts.fix: For each listed shift, reserve space for the culprit element: size images/embeds, preload web fonts, and insert dynamic content below the viewport or into pre-allocated containers.

lcp-breakdown-insight.why: This splits LCP into TTFB, load delay, load time, and render delay so you can target the slowest phase.
lcp-breakdown-insight.fix: Attack the longest phase: cut TTFB with caching/CDN, remove load delay by preloading the LCP image, speed load with better compression, and cut render delay by removing blocking CSS/JS.

lcp-discovery-insight.why: If the browser discovers the LCP image late (lazy-loaded or hidden from the preload scanner), LCP starts late no matter how fast it downloads.
lcp-discovery-insight.fix: Put the LCP image in the initial HTML with fetchpriority="high", remove loading="lazy" from it, and add <link rel="preload"> if it's referenced from CSS or JS.

legacy-javascript-insight.why: Polyfills and transforms for old browsers ship dead weight to the modern browsers most users have.
legacy-javascript-insight.fix: Target modern browsers in your build (browserslist/esbuild target es2020+), drop unneeded core-js polyfills, and use module/nomodule or serve modern-only bundles.

link-in-text-block.why: Links distinguished only by color are invisible to low-vision and colorblind users within body text.
link-in-text-block.fix: Keep underlines on in-paragraph links, or ensure a 3:1 contrast ratio against surrounding text plus a non-color cue.

link-name.why: Links without discernible text are unusable for screen reader users.
link-name.fix: Give icon-only links an aria-label or visually-hidden text; avoid "click here" as the only content.

link-text.why: Generic link text ("click here", "read more") gives screen reader users and search engines no context out of page flow.
link-text.fix: Use descriptive link text that makes sense out of context, e.g. "Read the pricing guide" instead of "click here".

list.why: Lists with invalid children break item counts and list navigation for screen readers.
list.fix: Ensure <ul>/<ol> directly contain only <li> (plus <script>/<template>); move other markup inside the <li>s.

listitem.why: <li> elements outside a list lose their list semantics and item position announcements.
listitem.fix: Wrap every <li> in a parent <ul>, <ol>, or <menu>.

long-tasks.why: Tasks over 50 ms block the main thread and delay input response, hurting TBT and INP.
long-tasks.fix: Split long tasks into smaller chunks that yield to the main thread, defer non-critical work, and offload computation to Web Workers.

mainthread-work-breakdown.why: Shows where main-thread time goes (script eval, style/layout, etc.) — a diagnostic, not a single fix.
mainthread-work-breakdown.fix: Cross-reference with bootup-time and unused-javascript; the biggest bucket usually points at the fix.

max-potential-fid.why: The longest main-thread task caps how badly a user's first input could be delayed.
max-potential-fid.fix: Split the longest tasks into chunks under 50 ms, defer non-critical JS, and move expensive computation off the main thread.

meta-description.why: Missing meta description hurts SEO snippet quality in search results.
meta-description.fix: Add a unique <meta name="description"> per page summarizing its content in ~150-160 chars.

meta-refresh.why: Timed meta refresh moves or reloads the page before users of assistive tech can finish reading.
meta-refresh.fix: Remove <meta http-equiv="refresh">; use a user-triggered link or server-side redirect instead.

meta-viewport.why: user-scalable="no" or a low maximum-scale blocks pinch-zoom, which low-vision users depend on.
meta-viewport.fix: Use <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1"> without user-scalable=no or maximum-scale below 5.

modern-http-insight.why: Requests over HTTP/1.1 lose multiplexing and header compression, adding latency on multi-request origins.
modern-http-insight.fix: Enable HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 on your server/CDN for all origins serving multiple resources.

modern-image-formats.why: JPEG/PNG are larger than AVIF/WebP for equivalent visual quality.
modern-image-formats.fix: Convert hero/content images to WebP or AVIF; most image pipelines (sharp, squoosh) do this at build time.

network-dependency-tree-insight.why: Long chains of critical requests (each waiting on the last) push out FCP and LCP.
network-dependency-tree-insight.fix: Shorten chains by inlining critical CSS, avoiding @import and JS-injected resources, and preloading late-discovered critical assets; add preconnect for critical third-party origins.

non-composited-animations.why: Animations that trigger layout/paint (not just transform/opacity) are janky and block the main thread.
non-composited-animations.fix: Animate transform/opacity only; move animated elements to their own layer with will-change or transform: translateZ(0) sparingly.

notification-on-start.why: Asking for notification permission on page load without context annoys users and usually gets denied or blocked.
notification-on-start.fix: Request Notification.requestPermission() only in response to a user action tied to a clear benefit, not on load.

object-alt.why: <object> embeds without alternate text are silent to screen readers, hiding the embedded content.
object-alt.fix: Add inner fallback text, aria-label, or title to every <object> element.

offscreen-images.why: Images below the fold loaded eagerly compete with critical resources.
offscreen-images.fix: Add loading="lazy" to below-the-fold , or use an Intersection Observer-based lazy loader.

origin-isolation.why: Without cross-origin isolation headers, the page is exposed to cross-origin attacks like Spectre-style leaks.
origin-isolation.fix: Send Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy: same-origin (and COEP where needed) to isolate the browsing context.

paste-preventing-inputs.why: Blocking paste in inputs breaks password managers and forces error-prone retyping, hurting usability and security.
paste-preventing-inputs.fix: Remove preventDefault() from paste event handlers (and any onpaste="return false") on input fields.

presentation-role-conflict.why: role="presentation"/"none" on elements that are focusable or carry ARIA attributes creates conflicting, unpredictable semantics.
presentation-role-conflict.fix: Remove role="presentation"/"none" from interactive elements, or drop the conflicting tabindex/ARIA attributes.

redirects.why: Each redirect hop adds a full round trip before the real response starts.
redirects.fix: Point links/canonical URLs directly at the final destination; fix trailing-slash or http->https redirect chains at the source.

redirects-http.why: Serving pages over HTTP or not redirecting to HTTPS leaves users on an insecure connection vulnerable to interception.
redirects-http.fix: Configure the server to 301-redirect all HTTP requests to HTTPS, and consider adding an HSTS header.

render-blocking-insight.why: Stylesheets and synchronous scripts in <head> block the first paint until they download and execute.
render-blocking-insight.fix: Inline critical CSS, load the rest with media/preload tricks, and add defer or async to scripts (or move them out of <head>).

render-blocking-resources.why: CSS/JS loaded synchronously in <head> delays first paint.
render-blocking-resources.fix: Inline critical CSS, defer/async non-critical scripts, use <link rel="preload"> for fonts, split large CSS bundles per route.

robots-txt.why: A malformed robots.txt can be misinterpreted by crawlers, accidentally blocking pages or ignoring your crawl rules.
robots-txt.fix: Validate robots.txt syntax (correct directives, one rule per line, valid user-agent groups) and fix the lines Lighthouse flags.

select-name.why: A <select> without a name is announced only as a combobox, with no hint what it chooses.
select-name.fix: Associate a <label for> with each <select>, or add aria-label/aria-labelledby.

server-response-time.why: Slow TTFB delays every subsequent render step.
server-response-time.fix: Cache at the edge/CDN, avoid blocking work in request handlers, use static generation (SSG) instead of SSR where content doesn't need to be dynamic.

skip-link.why: A skip link whose target doesn't exist or isn't focusable strands keyboard users instead of skipping navigation.
skip-link.fix: Point the skip link's href at an existing ID (e.g. <main id="main">) and ensure the target is focusable (add tabindex="-1" if needed).

speed-index.why: A high Speed Index means visible content fills in slowly, so the page looks incomplete for longer.
speed-index.fix: Inline critical CSS, defer render-blocking resources and offscreen images, and minimize main-thread work during load.

svg-img-alt.why: SVGs with role="img" but no accessible name are announced as unlabeled images.
svg-img-alt.fix: Add aria-label, aria-labelledby, or a <title> element (referenced via aria-labelledby) to <svg role="img">.

tabindex.why: tabindex values above 0 create a confusing custom tab order that jumps around the page.
tabindex.fix: Remove positive tabindex values; use only tabindex="0" or "-1" and rely on DOM order for focus sequence.

table-duplicate-name.why: A <caption> duplicating the table's summary attribute makes screen readers announce it twice.
table-duplicate-name.fix: Keep the <caption> and remove the duplicate summary text (or make them convey different information).

table-fake-caption.why: A styled cell used as a caption isn't announced as one, so screen reader users lose the table's title.
table-fake-caption.fix: Replace the fake caption cell with a real <caption> element inside the <table>.

target-size.why: Touch targets smaller than 24x24px (or crowded together) are hard to tap for users with motor impairments.
target-size.fix: Make interactive elements at least 24x24 CSS pixels or add spacing/padding so targets don't overlap neighbors.

td-has-header.why: Data cells in large tables without associated headers leave screen reader users guessing what a value means.
td-has-header.fix: Add <th> elements (with scope) or headers attributes so every <td> maps to its header.

td-headers-attr.why: headers attributes referencing IDs outside the same table associate cells with the wrong headers for screen readers.
td-headers-attr.fix: Ensure each <td headers> lists only IDs of <th> elements within the same <table>.

th-has-data-cells.why: Header cells that describe no data cells leave screen reader users with orphaned, meaningless headers.
th-has-data-cells.fix: Ensure every <th> (or role="columnheader"/"rowheader") relates to actual data cells; use scope="col"/"row" to clarify.

third-parties-insight.why: Third-party scripts (analytics, ads, widgets) consume network and main-thread time you don't control.
third-parties-insight.fix: Audit and remove low-value third parties, load the rest with async/defer or on interaction (facades for embeds), and set up preconnect for the essential ones.

third-party-cookies.why: Browsers are phasing out third-party cookies, so features depending on them will silently break for users.
third-party-cookies.fix: Remove reliance on third-party cookies or migrate to alternatives like CHIPS (partitioned cookies) or the Storage Access API.

third-party-summary.why: Third-party scripts (analytics, ads, widgets) often dominate main-thread time.
third-party-summary.fix: Load non-critical third parties with defer/async or after user interaction (e.g. facade pattern for embeds), audit whether each script is still needed.

total-blocking-time.why: Long main-thread blocks between FCP and TTI make the page unresponsive to clicks and keystrokes.
total-blocking-time.fix: Break up long tasks, code-split and defer non-critical JavaScript, remove unused JS, and move heavy work to Web Workers.

total-byte-weight.why: Large total page weight slows load especially on constrained networks.
total-byte-weight.fix: Audit largest resources in the network panel; compress images, code-split JS, remove unused fonts/icon sets.

trusted-types-xss.why: Without Trusted Types, any DOM sink (innerHTML etc.) is a potential DOM-based XSS entry point.
trusted-types-xss.fix: Send Content-Security-Policy: require-trusted-types-for 'script' and refactor sinks to use trusted type policies.

unminified-css.why: Unminified CSS wastes bytes on whitespace and comments, delaying render-blocking stylesheet downloads.
unminified-css.fix: Minify CSS in your build step (cssnano, esbuild, Lightning CSS) and serve the minified output with compression.

unminified-javascript.why: Unminified JS ships extra bytes that slow download, parse, and execution.
unminified-javascript.fix: Minify JS in your build (terser, esbuild, swc) and ensure production builds are what you deploy.

unsized-images.why: Images without explicit dimensions cause layout shifts when they load, hurting CLS.
unsized-images.fix: Add width and height attributes (or CSS aspect-ratio) to every and

unused-css-rules.why: Unused CSS is downloaded and parsed for nothing.
unused-css-rules.fix: Use Tailwind/PurgeCSS content scanning, remove dead component styles, avoid importing whole icon/UI libraries' CSS.

unused-javascript.why: Shipping JS the page never executes costs parse/compile time and bytes.
unused-javascript.fix: Code-split by route, lazy-load below-the-fold components, remove unused deps, check bundle with source-map-explorer/rollup-plugin-visualizer.

uses-responsive-images.why: Serving a larger image than the rendered size wastes bandwidth and delays LCP.
uses-responsive-images.fix: Use srcset/sizes or a framework's <Image> component (next/image, astro:assets) to serve device-appropriate sizes.

uses-text-compression.why: Uncompressed text assets (HTML/CSS/JS) inflate transfer size.
uses-text-compression.fix: Enable gzip or brotli at the server/CDN (most hosts do this automatically; verify headers with curl -I --compressed).

valid-lang.why: Invalid lang attributes on elements make screen readers mispronounce that section of content.
valid-lang.fix: Use valid BCP 47 codes in every lang attribute (e.g. lang="fr", not lang="french").

valid-source-maps.why: Missing or broken source maps make production errors and Lighthouse treemaps impossible to trace to source.
valid-source-maps.fix: Publish valid source maps for large first-party bundles (sourceMappingURL pointing at an accessible .map file).

video-caption.why: Videos without captions exclude deaf and hard-of-hearing users from the audio content.
video-caption.fix: Add a <track kind="captions" src="..." srclang="en"> element inside each <video>.

viewport-insight.why: Without a mobile viewport meta tag, browsers add a 300 ms tap delay and render a zoomed-out desktop layout.
